_metadata.description: Azure VM SKU family upgrade paths based on Microsoft official migration guidance
_metadata.usage: Keyed by SKU family+version (e.g., 'Dv2'). Each entry provides 3 upgrade recommendations: dropIn (lowest risk), futureProof (latest gen), costOptimized (AMD/alternative). The 'sizeMap' translates vCPU counts to specific SKU names for each path.

Av1.summary: Original A-series (A0-A11, Basic_A) retired. No direct successor; workloads should move to B-series (burstable) or D-series (general purpose).

dropIn.reason: Closest burstable replacement for entry-level/test workloads with low baseline CPU

futureProof.reason: General-purpose v5 provides consistent performance without burstable model; better for production workloads graduating from A-series

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based burstable series for lowest cost on dev/test workloads

Dv1.summary: Original D-series (D1-D14, DS1-DS14) retired. Microsoft recommends Dsv5 as drop-in, Dsv6 for future-proofing.

dropIn.reason: Same family, Premium SSD support, SCSI disk controller, Gen1+Gen2 OS support — minimal migration risk

futureProof.reason: Latest gen D-series with NVMe disk controller, higher IOPS/throughput, Emerald Rapids CPUs

costOptimized.reason: AMD EPYC-based D-series, typically lower cost for same vCPU/memory; good for stateless or non-CPU-sensitive workloads

Dv2.summary: Dv2/DSv2 series retiring March 2027. Microsoft recommends Dsv5 as primary replacement, Dsv6 for modernization.

dropIn.reason: Same family, same vCPU/memory ratios, SCSI disk controller, supports Gen1+Gen2 — safest migration from Dv2

futureProof.reason: Latest D-series with NVMe, higher storage IOPS, higher network bandwidth — best long-term investment

costOptimized.reason: AMD EPYC processors, often 5-15% lower cost than Intel equivalents for same vCPU/memory

Dv3.summary: Dv3/Dsv3 series retiring September 2027. Move to Dsv5 (safe) or Dsv6 (modern).

dropIn.reason: Direct successor with same vCPU/memory ratios, SCSI disk controller, Gen1+Gen2 support

futureProof.reason: NVMe disk controller, Emerald Rapids, significantly higher storage and network throughput

costOptimized.reason: AMD Genoa-based v6, latest AMD with NVMe, lower cost than Intel v6

Ev3.summary: Ev3/Esv3 series retiring September 2027. Memory-optimized workloads should move to Esv5 or Esv6.

dropIn.reason: Same memory-optimized ratios (8 GiB/vCPU), SCSI disk controller, Gen1+Gen2 — safest E-series migration

futureProof.reason: Latest E-series with NVMe, higher IOPS, Emerald Rapids — best for database and in-memory workloads long-term

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based memory-optimized, lower cost for databases and in-memory caching that are not CPU-sensitive

Fv1.summary: Fs-series (compute-optimized v1 with Premium SSD) retiring September 2027. Move to Fsv2 (already current) or Fasv6.

dropIn.reason: Direct compute-optimized successor, higher clock speed, well-established in all regions

futureProof.reason: AMD Genoa compute-optimized v6, strong single-thread performance at lower cost; Intel Fsv6 does not exist — Fasv6 is the only v6 F-series

Gv1.summary: G/GS-series (memory+storage optimized) retired. Workloads should move to E-series (memory) or M-series (large memory/SAP).

dropIn.reason: E-series provides equivalent memory-optimized ratios; closest replacement for G-series memory workloads

futureProof.reason: Latest memory-optimized with NVMe for long-term workload placement

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based E-series for memory workloads at lower cost

Hv1.summary: Original H-series (H8-H16r) retired. HPC workloads should move to HBv4 or HXv4.

dropIn.reason: HBv4 is the current HPC workhorse with AMD EPYC Genoa, InfiniBand NDR200, massive memory bandwidth

futureProof.reason: Large-memory HPC variant for workloads needing >200 GiB RAM with InfiniBand

costOptimized.reason: HBv3 still widely available and lower cost than v4 for HPC workloads that don't need NDR200

HBv1.summary: HBv1 (HB60rs) retired. Move to HBv4 for modern HPC.

dropIn.reason: HBv3 with 120 cores is the established HPC replacement with broad region availability

futureProof.reason: Latest HPC with AMD Genoa, NDR200 InfiniBand, highest memory bandwidth

costOptimized.reason: HBv3 offers strong HPC performance at lower cost than v4

HCv1.summary: HC44rs retired. Intel-based HPC workloads should consider HBv4 (AMD) or wait for HC-successor.

dropIn.reason: HBv3 provides comparable HPC capability; validation needed for Intel-specific HPC codes

futureProof.reason: Latest HPC generation, AMD Genoa with NDR200 InfiniBand

costOptimized.reason: HBv3 is the most cost-effective current HPC option

Lv1.summary: Ls-series (storage-optimized v1) retired. Move to Lsv3 or Lasv3.

dropIn.reason: Direct storage-optimized successor with NVMe local disks, higher IOPS and throughput

futureProof.reason: Lsv3 is currently the latest storage-optimized generation available

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based storage-optimized, lower cost for storage-heavy workloads

Mv1.summary: M-series v1 (large memory for SAP HANA) retiring August 2027. Move to Msv2/Mdsv2 or Msv3.

dropIn.reason: Msv2/Mdsv2 medium memory provides SAP-certified large-memory VMs with improved performance
requirements: Gen2 OS image for larger sizes

futureProof.reason: Latest M-series with NVMe, Sapphire Rapids, highest memory bandwidth for SAP HANA and large databases

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based M-series v3 for large-memory workloads at lower cost where Intel is not required

NCv1.summary: NCv1 (Tesla K80 GPU) retired. GPU compute workloads should move to NCv4 (AMD GPU) or NCadsA10v4.

dropIn.reason: A10 GPU provides strong inference and training capability as a modern replacement for K80

futureProof.reason: H100 GPU for AI/ML workloads — highest performance GPU compute available

costOptimized.reason: NVIDIA T4 GPU — lower cost for GPU inference and light compute

NCv2.summary: NCv2 (Tesla P100 GPU) retired. Move to NCadsA10v4 for inference or ND-series for training.

dropIn.reason: A10 GPU replaces P100 for both training and inference with better performance/watt

futureProof.reason: H100 GPU — top-tier for AI/ML training and inference at scale

costOptimized.reason: T4 GPU for cost-effective inference workloads

NCv3.summary: NCv3 (Tesla V100 GPU) retiring September 2025. Move to NCadsA10v4 or H100-based for AI/ML.

dropIn.reason: A10 GPU provides comparable GPU memory and better inference throughput than V100

futureProof.reason: H100 for heavy training workloads requiring maximum GPU compute

costOptimized.reason: T4 GPU extremely cost-effective for inference, rendering, and light training

NDv1.summary: NDv1 (Tesla P40 GPU) retired. AI training workloads should move to NDAMv4_A100 or NDv5 (H100).

dropIn.reason: A100 GPU is the standard AI training replacement, 10x+ performance over P40

futureProof.reason: H100-based for latest AI/ML training at maximum scale

costOptimized.reason: A10 GPU for lighter AI training/inference at significantly lower cost than A100

NDv2.summary: NDv2 (V100 with NVLink) retiring September 2025. Move to NDAMv4_A100 or NDv5 (H100).

dropIn.reason: A100 with NVLink successor, 3x training throughput over V100 per GPU

futureProof.reason: H100 NVLink for highest AI training performance, 6x over A100 for transformer models

costOptimized.reason: A100 is most cost-effective for multi-GPU training when H100 budget is prohibitive

NVv1.summary: NVv1 (Tesla M60 GPU) retired. GPU visualization workloads should move to NVadsA10v5 or NVv4.

dropIn.reason: A10 GPU for visualization with fractional GPU support, direct M60 replacement

futureProof.reason: NVadsA10v5 is the current latest visualization-class GPU series

costOptimized.reason: AMD Radeon MI25 — lowest cost GPU visualization for VDI and remote desktop

NVv3.summary: NVv3 (Tesla M60 refresh) retiring September 2025. Move to NVadsA10v5.

dropIn.reason: A10 GPU replaces M60 for visualization with better performance and fractional GPU support

futureProof.reason: NVadsA10v5 is the current latest NV-class series

costOptimized.reason: AMD Radeon MI25 for budget-friendly VDI and visualization

Av2.summary: A-series v2 is an older general-purpose family with no newer A-series successor. Workloads should migrate to D-series (general purpose) or B-series (burstable/dev-test).

dropIn.reason: General-purpose successor with consistent performance, Premium SSD, SCSI disk controller; safest migration from Av2

futureProof.reason: Latest D-series with NVMe, Emerald Rapids CPUs, higher IOPS — best long-term general-purpose platform

costOptimized.reason: Burstable v2 for dev/test workloads that don't need sustained CPU; lowest cost option for intermittent workloads

Bv1.summary: Original B-series burstable VMs. Bsv2 offers better baseline performance, more size options, and AMD alternatives.

dropIn.reason: Direct burstable successor with same credit-based model, improved baseline performance, more granular size options

futureProof.reason: General-purpose with consistent (non-burstable) performance for workloads that have outgrown burstable model

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based burstable v2 for lowest cost; same credit model with AMD EPYC processors

Fv2.summary: Fsv2 is the current compute-optimized workhorse family. While not yet retiring, Fsv6 offers higher single-thread performance and NVMe for future-proofing.

dropIn.reason: AMD Genoa compute-optimized v6; only v6 F-series available (Intel Fsv6 does not exist). Straightforward migration from Fsv2

Lv2.summary: Lsv2 is the storage-optimized v2 family with NVMe local disks. Lsv3/Lasv3 offer more size options; Lsv4 is the latest generation.

dropIn.reason: Direct storage-optimized successor with NVMe local disks, more granular size options, improved throughput per vCPU

futureProof.reason: Latest storage-optimized with higher NVMe throughput, more size options (down to 2 vCPU), best storage-to-compute ratio

costOptimized.reason: AMD-based storage-optimized with NVMe; lower cost per vCPU for storage-intensive workloads not sensitive to CPU vendor

Lv3.summary: Lsv3/Lasv3 storage-optimized v3. Lsv4 is the latest generation with broader size options and improved local storage performance.

dropIn.reason: Direct successor with same NVMe storage focus, broader size range (2-96 vCPU), improved per-vCPU throughput

futureProof.reason: Latest AMD-based storage-optimized with NVMe, extended size range, best price-performance for high-throughput storage

DCv2.summary: DCsv2 confidential compute VMs with Intel SGX enclaves. DCdsv3/DCdsv5 offer larger sizes and improved TEE capabilities.

dropIn.reason: Next-gen confidential compute with Intel TDX, larger size range (1-48 vCPU), supports both SGX and TDX isolation

futureProof.reason: Latest confidential compute with AMD SEV-SNP, broadest size range (2-96 vCPU), AMD EPYC processors

DCv3.summary: DCdsv3 confidential compute with Intel TDX. DCadsv5 is the latest AMD-based option with broader size range.

dropIn.reason: Latest confidential compute with AMD SEV-SNP, extended size range (2-96 vCPU), competitive pricing

NVv2.summary: NVv2 GPU visualization VMs with NVIDIA Tesla M60. NVv4 (AMD Radeon) and NVv5 (NVIDIA A10/V710) are newer options.

dropIn.reason: NVIDIA A10 GPU for visualization, broader size range, better GPU-to-vCPU ratio than NVv2

futureProof.reason: Latest NVIDIA V710 GPU for visualization, newest driver support, best long-term VDI platform

costOptimized.reason: AMD Radeon MI25 GPU for budget VDI — lower cost than NVIDIA options for standard visualization

HBv2.summary: HBv2 HPC VMs with AMD EPYC 7V12. HBv3 offers improved interconnect; HBv4 is the latest with AMD EPYC Genoa.

dropIn.reason: Direct HPC successor with AMD EPYC Milan, improved InfiniBand bandwidth, same 120-vCPU SKU model

futureProof.reason: Latest HPC with AMD EPYC Genoa (176 vCPU), NDR InfiniBand 400 Gb/s, highest memory bandwidth in HB family

HBv3.summary: HBv3 HPC VMs with AMD EPYC Milan. HBv4 is the latest generation with Genoa processors and NDR InfiniBand.

dropIn.reason: Latest HPC with AMD EPYC Genoa (176 vCPU), NDR InfiniBand 400 Gb/s, 50% more memory bandwidth than HBv3

Mv2.summary: Msv2/Mdsv2 memory-optimized VMs for large SAP HANA and in-memory databases. Mv3 offers broader size options and improved performance.

dropIn.reason: Latest memory-optimized with broader size range (16-416 vCPU), higher memory-to-vCPU ratio, NVMe support for SAP HANA and large databases

futureProof.reason: Latest M-series without local temp disk for cost savings on memory-optimized workloads with remote storage
